description: A type definition for graph elements (nodes or edges). Types are defined once in the world and referenced by ID from any element's type field. A type can describe what the type means, set default property values, and declare which properties are required for elements of this type.

id.description: Unique type identifier — referenced by elements via their type field

applies_to.description: Which element kind this type applies to (e.g. NODE, EDGE, DEVICE, FORMAT, THREAD). Tooling uses this to validate that a type is only used on the correct element kind.

description.description: What this type represents and how it should be used in the story world
examples: A sentient being that can act, speak, and have relationships. Characters drive the story through their choices and conflicts.
A spatial or containment relationship between two nodes (e.g., located-in, part-of, borders).

defaults.description: Default property values applied to elements of this type. Elements can override these in their own properties object.

required_properties.description: Property keys that elements of this type must define in their properties object

tags.description: Free-form tags for filtering and grouping types

examples.name: Character
examples.description: A sentient being that can act, speak, and have relationships.

examples.name: Spatial Relationship
examples.description: A spatial or containment relationship between two nodes.
